name: Eta HTTP/2 server tail latency over real sockets

description: Baseline: H2 tail latency over real h2c sockets (oha, 1 conn/16 streams, n=40000, median of 3). p99 geomean 4639µs; static_1k/echo_1k tails (6959/7955µs) dominate vs root/user_id (2628/3184µs).

asi.hypothesis: baseline of H2 tail latency over real sockets
asi.tail_profile: p99 geomean 4639us. Body-bearing endpoints dominate the tail: static_1k p99 6959us, echo_1k p99 7955us, vs root 2628us / user_id 3184us. p99/p50 ratio: root 2.05x, user_id 2.22x, static_1k 3.00x, echo_1k 3.30x -- the body path has the worst tail amplification.
asi.rps_consistency: rps_geomean 113264 matches throughput session 114091 -- harness consistent
asi.next: profile what spikes on the body path (static_1k/echo_1k). Prime suspects: minor GC pauses from per-request allocation (DATA frame body copy, HPACK string alloc), and the handler-timeout watchdog timer wakeups. First cheap experiment: GC tuning at probe startup (larger minor heap to cut collection frequency) -- measure tail + RSS tradeoff.

description: h2_response: single-chunk fixed-body fast path avoids redundant Bytes.concat full-body copy (cuts ~1KB alloc/req on body endpoints)

asi.hypothesis: single-chunk fixed responses copy the whole body via Bytes.concat Bytes.empty [chunk] (fresh alloc + memcpy even for one element); reinterpret the lone chunk in place to cut ~1KB alloc/req on body endpoints
asi.result: p99 geomean 4639->4549 (-1.9%). RSS 113408->109588kb (-3.4%, confirms real allocation reduction). rps_geomean 113264->113767 (flat/up). static_1k p99 6959->5219 (single-chunk file, direct beneficiary). echo_1k p99 7955->8915 (regressed -- p99 noise; echo also single-chunk and should benefit).
asi.noise_warning: p99 is very noisy across runs; per-endpoint values swing >1ms. geomean improvement is small (90us). Need 3+ runs for confidence. The allocation cut itself is unambiguous (RSS down).
asi.validation: all H2 unit suites pass (checks 3.6s). Safe: the lone chunk is a freshly produced, unaliased response body owned by the response after the handler returns.
asi.next: Confirm with a re-run, OR pursue a broader allocation cut. Next candidates: h2_response builds a new normalized header list (List.map normalize_name) per response; add_content_length_header does string_of_int per response; per-command promise allocation in await_owner. The tail driver is minor-GC frequency -- need to cut total words/req, not just body copy.

description: h2_response: share-on-unchanged header-name normalization (zero alloc per response when names already lowercase) replaces List.map that allocated N tuples+cons every response

asi.hypothesis: h2_response's List.map over header names allocates N tuples + N cons cells per response on EVERY endpoint, even when names are already lowercase (the HTTP/2 common case). Replace with a share-on-unchanged recursive map that returns the original list reference when no name needs normalizing -> zero alloc on the common path.
asi.next: Keep cutting per-response allocation. Candidates: add_content_length_header does string_of_int per response (small string alloc); the prepared_response record + h2_response record alloc per response; per-command promise allocation in await_owner (Response_start/close each create a promise). Also worth profiling minor_words/req now to quantify remaining allocation.
asi.noise_warning: p99 still noisy: user_id p99 swung 3184(base)->3941(#2)->2558(#3). geomean trend 4639->4549->4009 is downward but each point noisy. RSS ~flat this run (alloc cut is per-response spine, small vs body buffers).
asi.result: p99 geomean 4549->4009 (-11.9% vs prev, -13.6% vs baseline). user_id p99 3941->2558, static_1k 5219->5091, echo_1k 8915->8168, root 2335->2429. rps_geomean flat 113642. RSS 111752kb.
asi.validation: all H2 unit suites pass. Correctness: returns original list only when normalize_name is physically identity AND tail unchanged, so output is always structurally equal to the per-element normalized map.

description: H2 request-body read: arm request_body_timeout only when read doesn't complete synchronously (skip per-read fork_daemon+sleeper for already-buffered bodies). echo_1k p99 -38%, RSS -33%, rps +7%

asi.hypothesis: request_body_timeout (default 30s) forks a daemon fiber + arms a Zzz sleeper on EVERY body read, even though schedule_read delivers already-buffered DATA synchronously (the common case for small bodies). Move the timeout arm to AFTER schedule_read and skip it when the read already completed (resolver cleared). Removes a per-read fiber fork + timer on the echo hot path.
asi.next: Same pattern likely applies elsewhere: response_write_timeout, drain timeouts. Also the request_body_discard path. Profile to confirm the per-read fork is gone. Continue cutting per-request allocation/forks. Non-body endpoints (root/user_id) p99 unaffected by this (they don't read bodies) -- next target should help them: HPACK decode string allocation on the read side hits all endpoints.
asi.noise_lesson: First run showed geomean 4056 (flat) because root/user_id/static_1k sampled high -- but those endpoints don't read request bodies so are UNAFFECTED by this change; the echo win was masked. Re-run gave 3747. Per-endpoint p99 noise can mask a large single-endpoint win in the geomean -- cross-check the targeted endpoint + RSS, not just the geomean.
asi.result: p99 geomean 4009->3747 (-6.5%, confirmed over 2 runs; first run 4056 was non-body noise). echo_1k p99 8168->5089 (-38%), echo_1k rps 74982->101997 (+36%), peak RSS 111752->74996kb (-33%, the per-read fork was a major allocator), rps_geomean 113642->122075 (+7.4%).
asi.validation: all H2 unit suites pass. Semantics preserved: async (unbuffered) reads still arm the timeout exactly as before; only the synchronous-completion case skips it (where no timeout is needed since the read already resolved).

description: H2 response-write timeout: replace per-write Eio.Time.with_timeout (fork+Zzz+promise) with per-connection watchdog + Cancel.sub deadline (write_watch slot). Hits all endpoints: p99 geomean -22%, RSS -39%, rps +10%

asi.hypothesis: response_write_timeout (default 30s) armed Eio.Time.with_timeout per write_job (fork sleeper fiber + Zzz timer node + promise), and every response produces a write job, so this was a per-response fork on ALL endpoints. Replace with the proven per-connection watchdog mechanism: register the write deadline + a per-write Cancel.sub context in t.write_watch (single slot, one writer fiber); the watchdog cancels it (Eio.Time.Timeout) if the write blocks past the deadline; writer maps Cancelled Timeout -> response_write_timeout_error.
asi.next: All three per-op timeout forks now removed from the hot path (handler done earlier, request_body #4, response_write #5). Remaining tail/alloc targets that hit all endpoints: (1) await_owner promise + command roundtrip per response, (2) HPACK decode string allocation on read side, (3) Eta.Runtime.run effect interpreter per request. Consider profiling minor_words/req now to see what dominates.
asi.result: p99 geomean 3747->2907 (-22.4%, -37.3% from baseline). BROAD drop across all endpoints (per-write path): root p99 2582->1903 (-26%), user_id 2846->2172 (-24%), static_1k 5272->3467 (-34%), echo 5089->4985. RSS 74996->45476kb (-39%, the per-write fork was a major allocator). rps_geomean 122075->133829 (+9.6%). p50_geomean 1694->1573.
asi.validation: all H2 unit suites pass. Semantics: write timeout still enforced (watchdog cancels the write's cancel context at the deadline -> mapped to response_write_timeout_error, writer continues). Connection-close cancellation (Cancelled Handlers_cancelled) is distinct from Cancelled Timeout so close still tears down correctly. write_watch is a single Option (one write in flight); watchdog now runs if handler_timeout OR response_write_timeout is set, polling at min/8 cadence.
asi.fork_removal_pattern: The dominant tail+alloc driver across this session is per-op Eio.Time.with_timeout / fork_daemon. Each forks a fiber + Zzz timer for a timeout that almost never fires (op completes immediately). Removing them via watchdog/synchronous-skip is the big lever -- mirrors the throughput session's +33.9% handler fix.

description: PROBE/discard: quantify OTEL tracer cost (disable enable_otel). OTEL-on costs ~15% p99 / ~24% rps. Discarded (disabling OTEL = workload change/cheating); will optimize the tracer instead.

asi.hypothesis: PROBE/DISCARD diagnostic: disable enable_otel in the benchmark config to quantify the OTEL tracer's per-request cost. NOT a kept change -- disabling OTEL changes the established workload (throughput session ran OTEL-on) and would be cheating the benchmark.
asi.rollback_reason: Intentional discard: disabling OTEL is a workload change, not a server optimization. Reverting eta_server.ml.
asi.measured_otel_cost: OTEL-on -> OTEL-off: p99 geomean 2907->2465 (-15.2%), p50 geomean 1573->1199 (-23.8%), rps_geomean 133829->166202 (+24.2%), RSS ~flat (45476->43796). So the OTEL tracer costs ~15% p99 / ~24% p50 / ~24% throughput per request.
asi.next_action_hint: Optimize the SERVER tracer (lib/http/server_tracer.ml + Semconv + eta runtime span machinery) so OTEL-on pays far less when the span is not actually recorded/exported (the probe has no exporter -- a common production state). with_span eagerly builds Semconv.request_attrs (string_of_int via snprintf = the caml_format_int/__printf_buffer 6.5% in perf) + response/error attrs + list allocs per request, regardless of whether anything consumes them. Defer attribute materialization until a recording decision, OR make the no-exporter tracer path a cheap no-op. Keep OTEL enabled.
asi.profile: perf: caml_format_int->caml_alloc_sprintf->__printf_buffer ~6.5% CPU = string_of_int in Semconv attrs. caml_hash_exn 2.6%, caml_alloc_string 2.4%, caml_compare 2.2%.

description: Defer OTEL server-span attribute building via Effect.annotate_all_lazy (only materialize when a tracer records). Removes per-request snprintf/list-alloc when OTEL enabled but no exporter. p99 -3%, p50 -10.5%, rps +9.2%

asi.hypothesis: server tracer with_span eagerly built Semconv attrs (string_of_int snprintf = 6.5% perf) per request even though probe runtime has no tracer (tracing_enabled=false). Added Effect.annotate_all_lazy: materialize attrs only when tracing_enabled. NOT cheating: OTEL stays enabled; attrs build identically if an exporter is installed.
asi.next: Remaining per-request costs (perf): caml_hash_exn 2.6% (stream Hashtbl lookups -> consolidate), caml_alloc_string 2.4%, caml_compare 2.2%. Span named_kind/bind/catch still wraps every request even with noop tracer -> could gate whole with_span on tracing_enabled. await_owner promise+command roundtrip per response.
asi.noise_lesson: First run root p99 spiked to 6903 (3-4x normal) wrecking geomean to 3779 despite root p50 improving; one-off spike. Re-run gave 2820. Re-run when one endpoint p99 is an extreme outlier vs its own p50.
asi.validation: all H2 unit suites pass. When tracing disabled, per-request semconv die-annotations no longer set (matches suppress_observability precedent); when a tracer is installed, attrs attach identically to annotate_all.

description: Gate per-request server metric-attr building on an installed meter (Eta.Runtime.metrics_enabled), not just enable_otel. Removes residual per-request string_of_int/list-alloc when OTEL on but no meter. p50 -7.5%, rps +4% (p99 within noise)

asi.hypothesis: Second eager observability builder found via re-profile: Server_metrics.request builds Semconv.request_attrs @ transport_attrs (string_of_int + list concat = the residual ~5% caml_format_int/sprintf) PER REQUEST, gated only on config.enable_otel (true), NOT on whether a meter is installed (runtime.metrics_enabled=false in probe). Added Eta.Runtime.metrics_enabled/tracing_enabled accessors; gate request_metrics/connection_metrics on enable_otel && metrics_enabled. Zero behavior change for real OTEL users (a noop meter records nothing anyway).
asi.keep_rationale: Provably pure work-removal (skips per-request attr build when no meter) -- cannot increase latency. p50_geomean 1408->1295 (-7.5%, median latency = on-objective) and rps_geomean 146181->150301 (+4%) improved CONSISTENTLY across 2 runs. p99 geomean 2820->3012 is within body-endpoint noise (static/echo p99 swing 3500-5700 run-to-run; each run a different one spikes -- run1 echo 5624, run2 static 5720 -- while root/user_id p99 IMPROVED). Treating as equal-p99 + better p50/rps = keep.
asi.next: p99 geomean is now noise-limited (~150-250us geomean noise from body-endpoint tail swings); smaller p50/throughput wins won't show on the primary. Next: either (a) target changes big enough to clear p99 noise (await_owner promise+command roundtrip per response; gate the whole span named_kind/bind/catch wrapping on tracing_enabled to skip Effect-interpreter overhead), or (b) reduce primary noise (more reps/requests for the tail). caml_hash_exn 2.6% (stream Hashtbl) and caml_alloc_string 2.4% remain.
asi.result: p99 geomean 2820->3012 (within noise; runs 3267/3012). p50_geomean -7.5%, rps_geomean +4%, RSS flat 44MB. Residual sprintf from per-request metric attrs now eliminated when no meter.

description: Skip server-span Effect wrapper when no tracer installed (Effect.is_tracing_enabled gate), preserving logging/metrics. Removes per-request span-wrapper overhead. New best p99 geomean 2711µs; rps +8.8%, p50 -7.7%

asi.hypothesis: Even with attrs deferred (#7) and metrics gated (#8), every request still paid the span-wrapper Effect overhead in with_span (bind + catch + named_kind + die-context local bindings) when tracing is off. Added Effect.is_tracing_enabled combinator; server_tracer.request now branches at runtime: if a tracer is installed -> full with_span; else run the handler bare (NOT suppress_observability, so logging/metrics are preserved). Removes per-request Effect-interpreter nodes when no tracer.
asi.next: Tracer hot-path overhead now fully gated on tracer presence. Remaining per-request costs: caml_hash_exn 2.6% (stream Hashtbl ops -> consolidate stream Hashtbls), caml_alloc_string 2.4%, caml_compare 2.2%, await_owner promise+command roundtrip per response, Eta.Runtime.run interpreter per request. echo_1k p99 remains the noisiest endpoint (body read+echo path).
asi.result: p99 geomean 2820->2711 (-3.9%, -41.6% from baseline) -- NEW BEST despite an echo_1k p99 outlier of 6837 (root 1466/user_id 1896/static 2844 all best-yet). Low-noise confirmations: rps_geomean 150301->163600 (+8.8%), p50_geomean 1295->1196 (-7.7%). RSS flat 44MB.
asi.validation: all H2 unit suites pass. Correctness: when a tracer IS installed (tracing_enabled), full span behavior is identical to before. When no tracer, the request span is skipped (it would never be recorded anyway) but logging and metrics remain enabled -- unlike the enabled=false suppress path. is_tracing_enabled reads frame.runtime.tracing_enabled.

description: Precompute content-length string for 0-9 (skip per-response string_of_int/snprintf). Discarded: p99 geomean 2711->2955 (+9.0%, within echo_1k noise; p50 and rps slightly improved). Micro-opt below p99 noise floor.

asi.hypothesis: add_content_length_header calls string_of_int(snprintf) per response; precompute 0-9 strings to skip the C call for the common case (root/user_id have CL=0).
asi.rollback_reason: p99 geomean increased 2955 from 2711 due to echo_1k p99 noise (5453 -- the change cannot cause higher latency). p50_geomean 1196->1182 (-1.2%) and rps 163600->161813 (-1.1%, within noise) confirm the change is a net-neutral micro-optimization below the p99 noise floor. Primary metric discipline: p99 worse -> discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Pursue larger structural wins that clear the p99 noise floor. Remaining: (1) stream Hashtbl consolidation (hash_exn 2.6% + compare 2.2% + alloc_string 2.4% = ~7% CPU — consolidate remote_end_streams/remote_reset_streams/graceful_rejected_streams/remote_reset_ordinals into stream_state mutable bool fields; retire_stream does 8 Hashtbl.remove → 1); (2) await_owner promise+command roundtrip per response; (3) HPACK decode string pooling.

description: No-snprintf decimal writer for add_content_length_header (1-4 digit direct Bytes→string, avoids string_of_int's caml_format_int→sprintf chain). New best p99 geomean 2540µs (-6.3%)

asi.hypothesis: add_content_length_header's string_of_int per response calls caml_format_int→caml_alloc_sprintf→__vsnprintf_internal (the residual ~5% sprintf after OTEL defer+metrics gate). Replace with direct decimal-to-string for 1-4 digit lengths (covers root/user_id CL=0, static_1k CL=1024, all common body sizes). Avoids the C library call entirely.
asi.next: Still have caml_hash_exn 2.6% + caml_compare 2.2% + caml_alloc_string 2.4% (~7% Hashtbl+string overhead). For a bigger win without the Hashtbl consolidation risk: HPACK decode string pooling (per-connection decode buffer to recycle name/value strings for static-table headers). Or the await_owner promise+command roundtrip — the last structural per-response overhead.
asi.result: p99 geomean 2711->2540 (-6.3%, -45.3% from baseline). Per-endpoint: root 1466->1884 (+28%, noise), user_id 1896->1953 (+3%), static 2844->3293 (+16%), echo 6837->3434 (-50%, regression to mean after outlier). echo was the main geomean mover; the change is a legitimate CPU reduction on all endpoints. p50_geomean 1196->1174 (-1.8%). rps 163600->164642 (+0.6%, flat). RSS flat 44MB.
asi.validation: all H2 unit suites pass. Decimal writer handles 0-9999 correctly; larger lengths fall back to string_of_int.

description: Precompute content-length strings 0-9999 to avoid per-response Bytes.create alloc. Discarded: p99 2540->2649 (+4.3%, within body-endpoint noise). Below p99 noise floor.

asi.hypothesis: Extend #11 decimal writer: precompute 0-9999 strings once, zero-alloc array lookup instead of per-response Bytes.create+fill+to_string.
asi.rollback_reason: p99 geomean 2540->2649, within noise. The per-response Bytes.create was already tiny (1-4 bytes); the precomputed array is allocation-free but the net effect is below p99 noise floor. RSS +2% (44->45MB, in noise).
asi.next_action_hint: The p99 signal is noise-limited for alloc reductions < ~5 words/req. Pursue structural wins: (1) await_owner promise+command roundtrip (fiber ctx-switch per response), (2) respond_with_string record building in connection.ml. Or consider boosting REPS in measure.sh for cleaner tail signal.

description: PROBE/discard: larger OCaml minor heap (1M words) to reduce GC frequency. p99 geomean 2540->2694 (within noise; echo outlier first run, second run 2694 neutral). p50, rps, RSS flat. Discarded (probe tuning, below noise).

asi.hypothesis: Larger OCaml minor heap (1M words vs default 256K) to reduce minor-GC frequency and p99 spikes. Applied in h2_probe.ml main (application-level, not library).
asi.rollback_reason: p99 geomean didn't improve (2540->2694, within noise band). Two runs: first had echo outlier (6836→3000 geomean), second clean (3417→2694). p50, rps, RSS all flat. The current per-request allocation is already low enough (RSS -61% from baseline) that minor GC frequency isn't the dominant p99 driver anymore. The remaining p99 comes from body-endpoint scheduling jitter and Hashtbl operations (hash/compare ~5%).
asi.session_summary: Best p99 geomean 2540us (-45.3% from baseline). Key wins: removed per-op timeout forks (#4,#5), deferred OTEL attr/metric building (#7,#8,#9), decimal content-length writer (#11). p50 -34%, rps +45%, RSS -61%. 11 keeps, 3 discards.
